summary: Iteration 2 testing of MEMOBOT FX-PRO. Backend pytest: 42/42 PASS (4 new for /api/user/exchange-keys, 2 new for risk partial-PUT regression, 36 regression). The iter1 HIGH bug (partial PUT KeyError) is FIXED via defaults-merge in engines.py lines 53-54. New Binance per-user keys endpoint works correctly (paper↔live mode flip, masking, has_secret flag, auth required). Frontend: login red+gold MEMOBOT logo confirmed, sidebar compact logo + EN/AR toggle confirmed, AR click flips html dir to 'rtl' and shows Arabic 'لوحة التحكم', EN restores ltr. Dashboard copilot-card with mascot present. Settings → API Keys tab fully functional: input+save flips banner to 'Live mode', empty save reverts to 'Paper mode'. Trading 5-step pipeline executes successfully (Validation→Risk→Routing→Fill→Record). Bot start updates badge to RUNNING.

minor.issue: STILL NOT FIXED from iter1: CoinGecko 429 propagates as 500 (httpx.HTTPStatusError). Intermittent. test_signal failed once in this run with 500; passed on individual rerun. Suggest catching 429 in market_data.get_klines and returning cached/empty data.

issues: STILL PRESENT from iter1: React hydration warning '<span> cannot be a child of <option>'. A shadcn/Radix or similar control wraps option label in a <span> inside a native <option>. Low severity, but produces console errors on every page using the symbol picker.
STILL PRESENT from iter1: Recharts 'width(-1) and height(-1) of chart should be greater than 0' warnings on first render. Add minHeight/minWidth to ResponsiveContainer.

action_items: FIX (LOW, carryover from iter1): Wrap CoinGecko klines calls in market_data.py with try/except on 429 → return cached or empty list to prevent intermittent 500s on /strategies/{id}/signal, /market/indicators/*, /ai/sentiment/*.
FIX (LOW, carryover from iter1): Replace <span> wrapper inside native <option> elements (likely symbol picker) to silence hydration warnings.
FIX (LOW, carryover from iter1): Set min-height on Dashboard Cumulative PnL ResponsiveContainer.
critical_code_review_comments: engines.py: iter1 HIGH bug correctly fixed by merging defaults at lines 53-54 with `risk = {**risk_defaults, **risk_doc}` — partial PUTs no longer crash trade pipeline. Verified by new test TestRiskPartialPut.test_partial_put_then_preview_no_500.

server.py /user/exchange-keys: clean implementation, masking + has_secret pattern is correct, mode='paper' fallback when either field empty matches spec. Auth dependency works (fresh session GET returns 401).
server.py is now 831 lines — exceeds 700 line guideline; consider splitting into routers per domain (auth, trade, risk, market, reports, payments, exchange, lock, config).
Frontend i18n: AR translations correctly applied; document.documentElement.dir flip on toggle works; localStorage 'memobot_lang' persistence implied by toggle behaviour.
Backend .env: BINANCE_API_KEY/SECRET/PAYPAL_* present as empty placeholders — good (avoids breakage) but never used since per-user keys take precedence (correct design).
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (added TestExchangeKeys with 4 tests + TestRiskPartialPut with 2 tests)

success_rate.backend: 100% (42/42, with 1 known-flaky test passing on retry)

seed_data_creation: Tests created TEST_ prefixed user via /auth/register, exchange_credentials doc for admin (reset to empty at end), one strategy (deleted). Admin's bot was left RUNNING after frontend test (no destructive cleanup needed; can be stopped via Bot Control).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All iter2 features verified working. The only unresolved items are the 3 LOW-priority carryover issues from iter1 (CoinGecko 429 → 500, <span> in <option> hydration warning, Recharts width(-1) warning). New Binance keys endpoint behaves exactly per spec: PUT both empty → mode='paper'; PUT both non-empty → mode='live' with api_key_mask 'XXXX•••••XXXX'. Always reset with PUT {api_key:'', api_secret:''} at end of any keys test. Auth still uses Bearer header from login.access_token; httpOnly cookie also set so use a fresh requests.Session() for negative auth tests.
